common.siteDescription: Learning algorithms and data structures for web developers

home.heroDescription: Understanding algorithms and data structures is a key skill for developers. We help you master them through clear animations and step-by-step visualizations.
home.searchDesc: Search algorithms help find elements in data structures quickly and efficiently.
home.sortingDesc: Sorting algorithms arrange data in order — a fundamental skill in programming.

binary-search.title: Binary Search
binary-search.description: Binary search is an algorithm for finding an element in a sorted array by repeatedly dividing the search interval in half. At each step, the algorithm compares the middle element of the array with the target value and eliminates the half where the element cannot exist.
binary-search.whyItMatters: Binary search is a fundamental algorithm constantly used in web development: searching sorted data, autocomplete, database index lookups, implementing bisect functions. Understanding binary search helps you grasp how database indexes work and why they're so fast.

steps.initial: Initial state: sorted array and target value {target}
steps.compare: Comparing middle element arr[{mid}] = {value} with target {target}
steps.goRight: arr[{mid}] = {value} < {target}, excluding left half, searching right
steps.goLeft: arr[{mid}] = {value} > {target}, excluding right half, searching left
steps.notFound: Target element {target} not found in array

bubble-sort.title: Bubble Sort
bubble-sort.description: Bubble sort is a simple sorting algorithm that repeatedly walks through the array, compares adjacent elements, and swaps them if they are in the wrong order. The process repeats until the array is fully sorted.
bubble-sort.whyItMatters: While bubble sort is rarely used in production due to its inefficiency, it's an excellent learning algorithm for understanding sorting principles, element comparison, and value swapping. It's the foundation for understanding more complex sorting algorithms.

steps.passComplete: Pass {pass} complete. Largest element has 'bubbled up' to the end.

quick-sort.title: Quick Sort
quick-sort.description: Quick sort is an efficient sorting algorithm that uses a divide-and-conquer strategy. It selects a pivot element and partitions the array into two sub-arrays: elements less than or equal to the pivot, and elements greater than the pivot. The sub-arrays are then sorted recursively.
quick-sort.whyItMatters: Quick sort is one of the most widely used sorting algorithms in practice. It's the default sorting algorithm in many programming languages and standard libraries. Understanding quick sort teaches you the divide-and-conquer paradigm, which is fundamental to many efficient algorithms.

steps.noSwap: Element {value} at index {index} is in correct partition
